title: Rhode Island
excerpt:
Consumer Assistance in Rhode Island

The Rhode Island Consumer Assistance Program is run by the Rhode Island Parent Information Network, Inc. (RIPIN). Staff can answer questions about health insurance regulations, rights, and responsibilities. They can help you file a complaint or appeal a health plan decision. Get consumer help with a problem or question.

Medicaid in Rhode Island

You can apply for Medicaid in Rhode Island by contacting your state Medicaid agency.

Shop Health Insurance Marketplace® in Rhode Island

If your small business is located in Rhode Island, you can use the HealthSource RI website to learn more about offering your employees health coverage.

SHOP Health Insurance Marketplace® for Employees in Rhode Island

If your employer offers SHOP health coverage in Rhode Island, you should contact your employer or visit the HealthSource RI website for more information.

Health Insurance Marketplace® in Rhode Island

If you live in Rhode Island, you’ll use the HealthSource RI website to enroll in health coverage.

CHIP in Rhode Island

RIte Care covers uninsured children in families with moderate incomes that are too high to qualify for Medicaid. You can find out whether your children qualify for RIte Care right now. Fill out a Marketplace application and find out whether your children qualify. Learn more about RIte Care in Rhode Island.
